senses.definition: to strike someone repeatedly, usually causing physical harm or injury

examples.x: The bully threatened to beat the younger student if he didn't hand over his lunch money.
The police officer was accused of beating the suspect during the arrest.
In the movie, the hero beats the villain in a dramatic fight scene.
The soldiers were instructed not to beat prisoners during interrogation.
She feared he might beat her if he found out the truth.

senses.definition: to strike the palms of one's hands together forcefully, usually to show appreciation or to attract attention

examples.x: The audience clapped enthusiastically after the concert.
Children clapped with glee as the magician pulled a rabbit out of the hat.
The students clapped in rhythm to the music during the school assembly.
The spectators clapped and cheered as the marathon runners crossed the finish line.
Guests clapped politely at the end of the speech.

senses.definition: to pull something with effort along a surface

examples.x: She drags the suitcase across the airport floor.
The tractor easily drags the heavy logs through the forest.
He drags the garbage bin to the curb for pickup.
Right now, they are dragging the fallen branches to the burn pile.
The tow truck is dragging the stranded car to the repair shop.

senses.definition: to take someone or something suddenly or violently

examples.x: The police officer grabbed the suspect by the arm and pulled him away from the scene of the crime.
The lifeguard grabbed the struggling swimmer by the arm and pulled him to safety from the strong current.
The teacher grabbed the misbehaving student by the collar and escorted him out of the classroom.
In a fit of anger, he grabbed his coworker by the shoulders and shook him forcefully.
The coach grabbed the player by the jersey and pulled him aside for a private conversation.

senses.definition: to beat someone or something with a closed fist quickly and forcefully

examples.x: In a fit of anger, he decided to punch the wall, leaving a hole.
The boxer aimed to punch his opponent in the face to gain the upper hand.
Frustrated with the situation, she threatened to punch anyone who crossed her path.
The child playfully punched his friend in the arm during recess.
The martial artist practiced various techniques to punch with speed and precision.

senses.definition: to take someone's hand and move it up and down, mainly for greeting

examples.x: In a traditional ceremony, the newlyweds shook hands with each member of the wedding party.
The two business partners shook hands, sealing the deal on their collaboration.
As a sign of mutual respect, the leaders shook hands in front of the gathered diplomats.
The coach shook each player's hand before the crucial match, instilling confidence in the team.

senses.definition: to move the upper part of the body downward

examples.x: I had to bend at the knees to reach the low shelf.
The hiker bent at the waist to drink water from the stream.

senses.definition: to bend the head or move the upper half of the body forward to show respect or as a way of greeting

examples.x: As the queen entered the room, the courtiers bowed deeply.
Upon receiving the prestigious award, the actor bowed to acknowledge the audience's applause.
In many cultures, it is customary to bow when greeting elders as a gesture of politeness.
The orchestra members bowed in unison after their outstanding musical performance.
In the dojo, students were taught not only how to fight but also how to bow as a mark of mutual respect.

senses.definition: to bend from a straight position typically to rest the body against something for support
to sit, walk, or stand lazily with a downward head and rounded shoulders

examples.x: He tended to slouch in his chair during long meetings, which often gave the impression of disinterest.
After a tiring day, she would slouch on the sofa and watch TV for hours.
The teenager slouched down the street, hands in his pockets, lost in thought.

senses.definition: to support the weight of the body on a knee or both knees

examples.x: In prayer, the congregation was instructed to kneel and seek solace in their faith.
As a sign of chivalry, the knight would kneel before the king to pledge allegiance.
The athlete chose to kneel during the national anthem as a peaceful protest against social injustice.
During the ceremony, the participants were asked to kneel before receiving their awards.
In traditional weddings, the bride and groom often kneel at the altar during certain rituals.

senses.definition: to jump very high or over a long distance

examples.x: The athlete leaped over the high bar with impressive precision, setting a new record.
In the ballet performance, the dancer leaped across the stage, showcasing extraordinary grace and strength.
The mountain goat effortlessly leaped between rocky ledges as it navigated the steep mountain terrain.
During the freerunning routine, the athlete leaped from rooftop to rooftop with incredible agility.
In the long jump competition, the athlete leaped with all their might.

senses.definition: to walk slowly and carefully on one's toes

examples.x: Trying not to wake the baby, she tiptoed into the nursery.
The cat tiptoed silently through the room, stalking a small insect.
In the library, patrons are reminded to tiptoe to maintain a quiet atmosphere.
To avoid being noticed, he tiptoed past his colleague's desk in the office.
Attempting to sneak out of the house unnoticed, the teenager tiptoed down the stairs.

senses.definition: to move slowly with the body near the ground or on the hands and knees

examples.x: The baby began to crawl across the living room floor.
As the hikers reached the steep slope, they had to crawl on their hands and knees to navigate the challenging terrain.
In the dense underbrush, the jungle explorer needed to crawl to avoid entangling vines and thick foliage.
The soldier advanced cautiously, choosing to crawl through the muddy trench to avoid being seen by the enemy.
The cat stalked its prey and then began to crawl silently through the grass.

senses.definition: to put one's body in a flat position in order to sleep or rest

examples.x: I'm feeling tired, so I'm going to lie down for a while.
The doctor advised her to lie down and rest after her surgery.
The weary traveler lay down on the bed, eager to escape the hustle and bustle of the city.
The exhausted hiker lay down on the grass, relieved to finally be at rest.
The doctor advised him to lie down if he felt dizzy.

senses.definition: to open and close the eyes quickly and for a brief moment

examples.x: He had to blink several times to adjust to the sudden darkness.
When the wind blew, she couldn't help but blink to protect her eyes.
We blinked to adjust our eyes to the dim light.

senses.definition: to look at someone or something without blinking or moving the eyes

examples.x: She gazed out of the window, lost in thought as the rain fell softly outside.
The couple sat by the beach, gazing at the sunset painting the sky in vibrant hues.
The professor gazed at the students intently, expecting thoughtful responses to his question.
During the romantic dinner, they gazed into each other's eyes, sharing a silent connection.
The cat sat on the windowsill, gazing at the birds chirping in the garden with great interest.

senses.definition: to look with eyes half-opened when hit by light, or as a sign of suspicion, etc.

examples.x: In the bright sunlight, she squinted to shield her eyes from the glare.
The detective squinted at the unfamiliar figure in the dimly lit alley.
As he entered the dark room, he squinted to adjust his vision to the low light.
The child squinted while trying to read the small text on the distant sign.
She squinted at the menu in the dimly lit restaurant, struggling to read the options.

senses.definition: to look at someone or something without moving the eyes or blinking, usually for a while, and often without showing any expression

examples.x: I often stare at the night sky, contemplating the stars.
She stares at her computer screen for long hours during work.
The student is staring at the math problem, trying to solve it.
While I was in the waiting room, I stared at the clock.
Right now, I am staring at the intricate details of the painting.

senses.definition: to quickly open and close one eye as a sign of affection or to indicate something is a secret or a joke

examples.x: During the meeting, the colleague across the room winked to share a confidential message.
At the surprise party, everyone winked to maintain the secrecy of the celebration.

senses.definition: to laugh quietly and with closed lips

examples.x: The old man chuckled at the witty remark made by his friend.
He chuckled softly while reading the humorous passage in the book.
The comedian's clever wordplay had the audience chuckling throughout the performance.

senses.definition: to laugh in a light, silly, or often uncontrollable way as a result of nervousness or embarrassment

examples.x: The schoolgirls couldn't help but giggle when their teacher told a funny story.
She tried to hide her embarrassment but ended up giggling nervously.
The friends shared a secret joke, causing them to giggle uncontrollably.
During the awkward silence, the group began to giggle to break the tension.
The students giggled at the teacher’s accidental mispronunciation.

senses.definition: to give a half-smile, often displaying satisfaction, superiority, or amusement

examples.x: He couldn't hide his satisfaction and smirked at the success of his plan.
The mischievous child smirked after playing a clever prank on his sibling.
The villain in the movie smirked as his evil plot unfolded.

senses.definition: to walk firmly with regular steps

examples.x: The soldiers marched in perfect formation, their boots hitting the ground with a rhythmic cadence.
The police officers marched down the street, ensuring a visible presence during the community event.
The athletes marched proudly into the stadium during the opening ceremony of the sports competition.
They marched together, singing songs of unity.

senses.definition: to move one's head up and down as a sign of agreement, understanding, or greeting

examples.x: He nodded to greet his neighbor as he walked by.
She nodded in agreement with his statement.
The audience nodded in understanding as the speaker explained the concept.
The teacher nodded approvingly at the student's answer.

senses.definition: to walk back and forth in a small area at a fixed speed, often due to anxiety or being deep in thought

examples.x: The nervous father paced outside the delivery room, awaiting news of his newborn.
Frustrated by the delay, she began to pace around the train platform.
Unable to sit still, he paced back and forth in his office while waiting for the important phone call.
The stressed-out student paced around the room, trying to memorize facts before the big exam.

senses.definition: to slip or hit something with the foot accidentally that makes one fall or lose balance momentarily

examples.x: She didn't notice the small step and tripped as she entered the dark room.
Carrying a stack of books, he tripped on the rug and scattered the books across the floor.
The loose shoelace caused him to trip on the staircase, catching everyone's attention.
Excitedly running to catch the bus, she tripped on the curb and scraped her knee.

senses.definition: to make one's fingers V-shaped and put them behind a person's head as a way of joking, particularly when taking a photograph

examples.x: I can't believe you gave me bunny ears in that group photo!

senses.definition: to sit on one's calves and move the chest close to one's knees

examples.x: She crouches down to pick up the fallen papers from the floor.
The baseball player crouched low, ready to field the ball.
The hiker crouched behind a rock to hide from the approaching storm.
They were crouching in the bushes, observing the wildlife.

senses.definition: to become conscious again after sleeping

examples.x: After a refreshing nap, it takes a moment to fully wake and regain awareness.
Parents often check on their children when they wake in the middle of the night.
Some people naturally wake early, while others are night owls who prefer to stay awake later.
She prefers to wake naturally without the use of an alarm clock on weekends.
